scenario: akm-task adapter recognize(component, file) for every file in tests/fixtures/bundles/akm-task/. .yml tasks derive type=task; conceptId strips .yml. Recognition does not gate on validity. SPECIFICATION golden.
adapterStatus: NOT IMPLEMENTED — spec-authored target the future akm-task adapter must reproduce (future Chunk-2 format-adapter work item).
specSource: docs/design/akm-0.9.0-bundle-adapter-spec.md §7 (akm-task adapter row: native task-YAML format, export facet), §6 (task row: '.yml under tasks/ (not OKF markdown)'), §1.3 (conceptId = path − ext).
realWorldSource: AKM-native format — grounded in the existing task schema (src/tasks/) and the frozen all-types stash fixture tests/fixtures/stashes/all-types/tasks/all-types-task.yml (schedule/enabled/prompt)
notes: SPECIFICATION golden — no dedicated akm-task adapter module exists (task recognition currently lives in the akm matcher stack: matchers.ts DIR_TYPE_MAP tasks → task on ext==='.yml'). Tasks are AKM-native YAML, NOT OKF markdown concepts.
conceptId strips the .yml extension (mirrors the frozen placement golden's taskSpec).
The invalid task (two-targets.yml) is still RECOGNIZED (type=task) — the invalid-task-yaml violation is a validate()/lint concern (see lint.json), not a recognition gate.

nightly-index.yml.name: nightly-index
nightly-index.yml.derivation: .yml task → type=task (spec §6 task row). Conformant: schedule + enabled + exactly one target (prompt).

two-targets.yml.name: two-targets
two-targets.yml.derivation: .yml task → type=task. RECOGNIZED despite declaring two targets; the violation is reported by validate() (see lint.json).
